steps.title: Cancel and split the absolute values
steps.content:
Subtract $4$ from both sides. If $|A|=|B|$, then $A=B$ or $A=-B$, so
$$x+4=2x-17\quad\text{or}\quad x+4=-(2x-17)$$

steps.title: Solve both linear equations
steps.content:
The first equation gives $x=21$. The second gives
$$x+4=-2x+17\Rightarrow 3x=13\Rightarrow x=\frac{13}{3}$$
Since $\frac{13}{3}<21$, the smallest solution is $\frac{13}{3}$.
